source:
#!/usr/bin/env python
# coding: utf-8

# # Row Removal Analysis — L0 Output & L3 Delta
#
# Counts:
#   1. Empty rows in L0 output  (all-null or blank critical fields)
#   2. Duplicate rows in L0 output
#   3. Duplicate hash_ids removed in L3 Delta
#   4. Null hash_id rows removed in L3

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession, Window
from pyspark.sql.functions import (
    col, trim, lit, count, when,
    row_number, desc, isnan, lower,
    concat_ws, md5, coalesce
)
from pyspark.sql.types import StringType
from notebookutils import mssparkutils

# ── Config — adjust to your environment ──────────────────────────────────────
env             = "stg"
storage_account = f"baubaisadfsa{env}"

# L0 source data (206,320 rows per SQL query)
l0_parquet_path = (
    f"abfss://juror-etl@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/transformation"
)

# Final output after transformation (205,774 rows per SQL query)
l3_delta_path = (
   f"abfss://dl-juror-eric-voters-temp@{storage_account}.dfs.core.windows.net/voters_deduplicated_delta"
)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

spark = SparkSession.builder \
    .appName("L0_L3_Removal_Analysis") \
    .config("spark.sql.extensions",
            "io.delta.sql.DeltaSparkSessionExtension") \
    .config("spark.sql.catalog.spark_catalog",
            "org.apache.spark.sql.delta.catalog.DeltaCatalog") \
    .getOrCreate()


# ================================================================================
# HELPERS
# ================================================================================

def separator(title):
    print("\n" + "=" * 65)
    print(f"  {title}")
    print("=" * 65)




def read_l0_files(base_path):
    """
    Discover the actual parquet files under base_path.
    The /files folder may contain subfolders (parquet/, csv/, excel/).
    We walk the tree, collect all .parquet paths, and read them directly.
    """
    from notebookutils import mssparkutils

    def _ls_recursive(path, depth=0):
        found = []
        if depth > 5:
            return found
        try:
            for item in mssparkutils.fs.ls(path):
                if item.isDir:
                    found.extend(_ls_recursive(item.path, depth + 1))
                else:
                    if item.path.lower().endswith(".parquet"):
                        found.append(item.path)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  Warning: cannot list {path}: {e}")
        return found

    parquet_paths = _ls_recursive(base_path)
    print(f"\nParquet files found under {base_path}: {len(parquet_paths)}")

    if not parquet_paths:
        # print top-level contents to help diagnose
        print("  Nothing found — listing top-level contents:")
        try:
            for item in mssparkutils.fs.ls(base_path):
                print(f"    {'dir' if item.isDir else 'file'}: {item.path}")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  Cannot list base path: {e}")
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"No .parquet files found at {base_path}. "
            "Check the path or verify L0 has run."
        )

    # Read each file individually and union on lowercase column names
    # to avoid COLUMN_ALREADY_EXISTS when files have mixed-case column names
    # (e.g. some files have "PostCode", others have "postcode")
    from functools import reduce
    from pyspark.sql import DataFrame

    def _normalise_cols(df):
        """Lowercase all column names."""
        for c in df.columns:
            df = df.withColumnRenamed(c, c.lower())
        return df

    dfs = []
    for i, path in enumerate(parquet_paths):
        try:
            dfs.append(_normalise_cols(spark.read.parquet(path)))
        except Exception as e:
            fname = path.split("/")[-1]
            print(f"  Warning: skipping {fname}: {e}")

    if not dfs:
        raise RuntimeError("All parquet files failed to read.")

    # Union on common columns
    common_cols = sorted(set(dfs[0].columns))
    for d in dfs[1:]:
        common_cols = [c for c in common_cols if c in d.columns]

    df = reduce(DataFrame.union, [d.select(common_cols) for d in dfs])
    print(f"  -> {df.count():,} rows loaded across {len(dfs)} files ({len(common_cols)} common columns)")
    return df


# ================================================================================
# SECTION 1 — L0 OUTPUT: EMPTY ROWS
# ================================================================================
separator("L0 OUTPUT — EMPTY ROW ANALYSIS")

L0_CRITICAL_FIELDS = ["elector number", "elector surname", "la_code"]

df_l0 = read_l0_files(l0_parquet_path)
df_l0.cache()
total_l0 = df_l0.count()
print(f"\nTotal rows in L0 output : {total_l0:,}")

# 1a. Rows where EVERY column is null (completely blank rows)
all_null_cond = " AND ".join([f"`{c}` IS NULL" for c in df_l0.columns])
fully_empty_rows = df_l0.filter(all_null_cond).count()
print(f"Fully empty rows (all columns null): {fully_empty_rows:,}")

# 1b. Per critical field
for field in L0_CRITICAL_FIELDS:
    if field in df_l0.columns:
        n = df_l0.filter(
            col(f"`{field}`").isNull() | (trim(col(f"`{field}`")) == "")
        ).count()
        print(f"  Rows with empty '{field}': {n:,}")

# 1c. Any critical field empty (union of above — what L1 will remove)
present_critical = [f for f in L0_CRITICAL_FIELDS if f in df_l0.columns]
empty_any_critical = 0
if present_critical:
    or_condition = " OR ".join(
        [f"(`{f}` IS NULL OR TRIM(`{f}`) = '')" for f in present_critical]
    )
    empty_any_critical = df_l0.filter(or_condition).count()
    pct = round(empty_any_critical / total_l0 * 100, 2) if total_l0 > 0 else 0
    print(f"\nRows removable (any critical field empty): {empty_any_critical:,}  ({pct}%)")
else:
    print("\nCritical fields not found in schema — skipping critical-field check.")


# ================================================================================
# SECTION 2 — L0 OUTPUT: DUPLICATE ROWS
# ================================================================================
separator("L0 OUTPUT — DUPLICATE ROW ANALYSIS")

# Dedup key mirrors optimise_deduplicate_data() / hash_id generation logic
DEDUP_KEY_CANDIDATES = {
    "register": ["register_lett", "reg_lett"],
    "forename": ["elector forename", "fname"],
    "surname":  ["elector surname",  "lname"],
    "rec_num":  ["rec_num",          "la_code"],
}

existing_cols = set(df_l0.columns)
dedup_key = []
for field_type, options in DEDUP_KEY_CANDIDATES.items():
    for opt in options:
        if opt in existing_cols:
            dedup_key.append(opt)
            break

if not dedup_key and "Elector Number" in existing_cols:
    dedup_key = ["Elector Number"]

print(f"\nDeduplication key fields : {dedup_key}")

duplicate_rows_l0 = 0
if dedup_key:
    df_deduped_l0  = df_l0.dropDuplicates(dedup_key)
    unique_rows_l0 = df_deduped_l0.count()
    duplicate_rows_l0 = total_l0 - unique_rows_l0
    pct = round(duplicate_rows_l0 / total_l0 * 100, 2) if total_l0 > 0 else 0

    print(f"Total rows               : {total_l0:,}")
    print(f"Unique rows (after dedup): {unique_rows_l0:,}")
    print(f"Duplicate rows removed   : {duplicate_rows_l0:,}  ({pct}%)")

    if duplicate_rows_l0 > 0:
        print("\nTop 10 most-duplicated key combinations:")
        df_l0.groupBy(*[f"`{k}`" for k in dedup_key]) \
             .count() \
             .filter(col("count") > 1) \
             .orderBy(col("count").desc()) \
             .limit(10) \
             .show(truncate=False)
else:
    print("Could not determine dedup key — skipping duplicate analysis.")


# ================================================================================
# SECTION 3 — L3 DELTA: DUPLICATE hash_id & NULL hash_id
# ================================================================================
separator("L3 DELTA — DUPLICATE hash_id ANALYSIS")

null_hash_count     = 0
dup_hash_count      = 0
removed_by_dedup_l3 = 0

# voters_postgresql is written as parquet (not delta) by the pipeline
try:
    df_l3 = spark.read.format("delta").load(l3_delta_path)
    print("  Read as Delta table")
except Exception:
    df_l3 = spark.read.option("mergeSchema", "true").parquet(l3_delta_path)
    print("  Read as Parquet")
total_l3 = df_l3.count()
print(f"\nTotal rows in L3 Delta table : {total_l3:,}")

if "hash_id" in df_l3.columns:

    # 3a. Null hash_ids
    null_hash_count = df_l3.filter(col("hash_id").isNull()).count()
    print(f"Rows with NULL hash_id       : {null_hash_count:,}")

    # 3b. Duplicate hash_ids
    distinct_hash_count = df_l3.select("hash_id").distinct().count()
    dup_hash_count = total_l3 - distinct_hash_count
    pct = round(dup_hash_count / total_l3 * 100, 2) if total_l3 > 0 else 0
    print(f"Distinct hash_ids            : {distinct_hash_count:,}")
    print(f"Duplicate hash_id rows       : {dup_hash_count:,}  ({pct}%)")

    if dup_hash_count > 0:
        print("\nTop 10 most-duplicated hash_ids:")
        df_l3.groupBy("hash_id") \
             .count() \
             .filter(col("count") > 1) \
             .orderBy(col("count").desc()) \
             .limit(10) \
             .show(truncate=False)

    # 3c. Simulate L3 dedup — keep most recent record per hash_id
    #     Priority order mirrors deduplicate_by_hash_id() in L3 notebook
    order_cols = []
    for c in ["AdtclmnRecordActiveInd", "AdtclmnModifiedDatetime",
              "AdtclmnFirstCreatedDatetime", "creation_date"]:
        if c in df_l3.columns:
            order_cols.append(desc(c))

    if not order_cols:
        order_cols = [col("hash_id")]

    window_spec   = Window.partitionBy("hash_id").orderBy(*order_cols)
    df_l3_deduped = (
        df_l3.withColumn("_rn", row_number().over(window_spec))
             .filter(col("_rn") == 1)
             .drop("_rn")
    )
    after_dedup_l3      = df_l3_deduped.count()
    removed_by_dedup_l3 = total_l3 - after_dedup_l3

    print(f"\nAfter L3 deduplication       : {after_dedup_l3:,}")
    print(f"Rows removed by dedup        : {removed_by_dedup_l3:,}")

else:
    print("hash_id column not found in L3 Delta table.")


# ================================================================================
# SECTION 4 — COMBINED SUMMARY
# ================================================================================
separator("COMBINED REMOVAL SUMMARY")

# Known counts from SQL OPENROWSET queries
KNOWN_SOURCE = 206_320   # juror-etl/transformation/**
KNOWN_OUTPUT = 205_774   # juror-etl/voters_postgresql/**
known_diff   = KNOWN_SOURCE - KNOWN_OUTPUT

print(f"\nKnown counts (from SQL OPENROWSET):")
print(f"  Source  juror-etl/transformation    : {KNOWN_SOURCE:>10,}")
print(f"  Output  juror-etl/voters_postgresql : {KNOWN_OUTPUT:>10,}")
print(f"  Difference                          : {known_diff:>10,}  <- rows to account for")

summary = [
    ("L0 source",    "Fully empty rows (all columns null)", fully_empty_rows),
    ("L0 source",    "Empty critical field rows",           empty_any_critical),
    ("L0 source",    "Duplicate rows (by dedup key)",       duplicate_rows_l0),
    ("Final output", "Null hash_id rows",                   null_hash_count),
    ("Final output", "Duplicate hash_id rows",              dup_hash_count),
]

total_accounted = sum(c for _, _, c in summary)
unaccounted     = known_diff - total_accounted

print(f"\n{'Layer':<14} {'Reason':<44} {'Rows':>10}")
print("-" * 70)
for layer, reason, cnt in summary:
    print(f"{layer:<14} {reason:<44} {cnt:>10,}")
print("-" * 70)
print(f"{'Accounted total':<58} {total_accounted:>10,}")
print(f"{'Known diff':<58} {known_diff:>10,}")
print(f"{'Unaccounted (L1/L2 business rule removals)':<58} {unaccounted:>10,}")

if unaccounted > 0:
    print(f"\n  -> {unaccounted:,} rows were removed by L1/L2 filters")
    print("     (empty surname/elector number/LA_Code, special-chars-only fields,")
    print("      null postcode, empty address — see validation_rules.json)")
elif unaccounted < 0:
    print(f"\n  Warning: categories overlap — recheck counts.")
else:
    print("\n  All missing rows fully accounted for.")

print("\nDone.")
